description: Decision Model v1.1 Confidence Semantics with three-dimensional judgment calibration. Validates alignment + completeness + evidence_quality framework through adversarial fixtures.

inheritance.note: v1.1 adds confidence semantics as additive layer; all v1.0 fields remain unchanged

confidence_semantics.name: Unknowns extracted as first-class reasoning objects

calibration_validation.name: High-Fit-Low-Upside fixture validated (perfect fit, incomplete data)
Medium-Fit-High-Trajectory fixture validated (skill gap, high growth signal)

pattern_stability.name: Completeness + Evidence Quality → Confidence formula consistent across fixtures
Confidence scores in MEDIUM range (0.45-0.55) align with human uncertainty
Unknowns list correctly identifies decision-material missing factors

fixtures.name: principal-servicenow-role
fixtures.scenario: Perfect skill match, incomplete company/team/compensation data

fixtures.name: high-fit-low-upside
fixtures.scenario: Perfect alignment, but declining company, below-market comp
fixtures.pattern_insight: Perfect alignment, low completeness (43%) → honest uncertainty (0.45)

fixtures.name: medium-fit-high-trajectory
fixtures.scenario: Skill/level gap, but hot company with leadership opportunity
fixtures.pattern_insight: Moderate alignment, moderate completeness (57%) + mixed quality → calibrated uncertainty (0.55)

calibration_summary.key_finding: v1.1 separates alignment (objective measurement) from confidence (subjective interpretation). Confidence scores now reflect honest uncertainty rather than false precision.

governance.v1_1_additive_policy: New v1.1 fields are optional in output; all v1.0 fields remain mandatory for backward compatibility

domain_validation.description: Validate that engine generalizes beyond golden fixtures (deferred to Phase 4)
criteria: Behavior degrades gracefully with missing data

to_advance_to_certified: ✓ No new decision logic changes (evaluation frozen)
✓ Certification artifact with fixtures + validation reviews archived
advancement_criteria.recommended_action: Advance to 'Judgment Calibration v1.1 Certified' after Phase 3 review and stakeholder sign-off
